MOVING FROM AI-FIRST TO AI-NATIVE
articles.summary: AI-First layers AI onto existing processes, while AI-Native rebuilds workflows from first principles with AI at the core. Success requires cultural shifts, role redesign, specialized agents, and embedding AI skills across the organization.

articles.summary: Urgent requests test leadership, not just speed. Strong product managers pause to re-scope, communicate, and calibrate, execute with focus and allies, and then recover by repairing relationships and reflecting for future resilience.

articles.title: STOP TRYING TO MAKE PRIORITIZATION "EASY"
articles.summary: Prioritization is meant to be hard - it's the balance of ambition, capacity, and investment. Over-optimizing any single dimension creates dysfunction, while continuous dialogue across all three ensures focus, adaptability, and impact.

articles.summary: Leaders must go beyond fast shipping to find problem-solution fit and founder fit. Mellinger's three-phase framework - incubate, immerse, and integrate - creates stronger teams, sharper insights, and products people actually adopt.

articles.title: REVERSE IMPOSTOR SYNDROME
articles.summary: Reverse imposter syndrome happens when you know you're skilled but lack external recognition, leaving perception misaligned with reality. Overcoming it requires shifting focus from doing great work to positioning it visibly and strategically.
